source_intake_record.raw_question_text:
A cube with the edge $3 \mathrm{~cm}$ long is painted grey and cut into smaller cubes each with an edge of $1 \mathrm{~cm}$ long. How many smaller cubes will have exactly 2 faces painted?
<image1>

image_dependency: requires_image=false indicates the problem should be solvable from text alone
text_dominant=true supports low reliance on visual content
The question text fully states the cube dimensions and painting/cutting setup without needing a diagram
asset_registry_record.issues includes 'question_text contains an <image1> placeholder', so image dependence cannot be scored as zero
multi_step: Solving requires interpreting the painted-cube setup and identifying which small cubes have exactly 2 painted faces
A typical solution combines at least two ideas: cubes with exactly 2 painted faces lie on edges but not corners, then count them
This is not a direct lookup; it involves geometric counting reasoning even though the final computation is short
verifiability: answer_type is numeric and answer_text is a single clear value: 12
The problem has a standard checkable rule for painted cubes, so the answer can be independently verified
registry_passed=true and both question and answer are present in the manifests

decision_reasons: question, answer, and primary image are all present, so core semantic assets are available
verifiability is high (0.93) and the answer is a clear numeric value
image dependency is low (0.12) because the problem appears solvable from text alone
the <image1> placeholder is a minor asset-quality issue, but it does not remove recoverable core meaning
